problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'test-social-network'. Detect if any deployment has zero replicas running, which would cause service unavailability. Affected: Pods like user-service-5b549cc8dc-4wfmd, namespace test-social-network, service user-service. Symptoms: No pods available for service user-service' in service endpoints. Events show 'Scaled to 0 replicas. No pods available for service user-service.
attempted_solutions: kubectl get events -n test-social-network --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' --field-selector type!=Normal | tail -50
kubectl get deployment user-service -n test-social-network -o yaml
final_solution:
kubectl scale deployment user-service --replicas=1 -n test-social-network
kubectl get pods -n test-social-network -l app=user-service
kubectl get deployment user-service -n test-social-network -o wide
kubectl describe pod user-service-5b549cc8dc-4wfmd -n test-social-network
kubectl logs user-service-5b549cc8dc-4wfmd -n test-social-network --tail=50
kubectl get endpoints user-service -n test-social-network
kubectl get events -n test-social-network --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' --field-selector type!=Normal | tail -50
reasoning: Root cause: The user-service deployment has been scaled to zero replicas due to an accidental scaling operation or a misconfigured autoscaler, causing the service to be unavailable.
symptoms: No pods available for service user-service' in service endpoints
No pods available for service user-service
outcomes: Deployment scaled; pods available
No further error events in affected namespace
prevention: Verify nodeSelector/affinity labels exist on target nodes before deploy. Use PreferredDuringScheduling unless strict isolation is required. Set accurate resource requests so the scheduler can place pods.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'astronomy-shop'. Identify the service(s) where the root cause of the fault lies. Affected: Pod recommendation-bb8cffcd8-qk72v, namespace astronomy-shop, service recommendation, Valkey service valkey-cart. Symptoms: This is caused by an incorrect CACHE_ADDR environment variable pointing to a non-existent Valkey instance. Connection refused. Valkey connection failed.
final_solution:
kubectl exec recommendation-bb8cffcd8-qk72v -n astronomy-shop -- env | grep -iE 'redis|cache|valkey'
kubectl exec recommendation-bb8cffcd8-qk72v -n astronomy-shop -- python -c 'import os; print("CACHE_ADDR:", os.getenv("CACHE_ADDR", "NOT_SET")); print("REDIS_ADDR:", os.getenv("REDIS_ADDR", "NOT_SET")); print("All env vars with cache/redis:"); import sys; [print(k, "=", v) for k, v in os.environ.items() if "cache" in k.lower() or "redis" in k.lower() or "valkey" in k.lower()]'
kubectl get configmap -n astronomy-shop | grep -i recommendation
kubectl get deployment recommendation -n astronomy-shop -o yaml
kubectl get service recommendation -n astronomy-shop -o yaml
kubectl get endpoints valkey-cart -n astronomy-shop -o yaml
kubectl exec recommendation-bb8cffcd8-qk72v -n astronomy-shop -- python -c
kubectl set env deployment/recommendation CACHE_ADDR=valkey-cart.valkey-cart.svc.cluster.local -n astronomy-shop
kubectl rollout restart deployment/recommendation -n astronomy-shop
kubectl get pods -n astronomy-shop
kubectl describe pod recommendation-bb8cffcd8-qk72v -n astronomy-shop
kubectl logs recommendation-bb8cffcd8-qk72v -n astronomy-shop --tail=50
reasoning: Root cause: The recommendation service is unable to connect to the Valkey cache, leading to failed product recommendations and increased latency in the recommendation endpoint. This is caused by an incorrect CACHE_ADDR environment variable pointing to a non-existent Valkey instance.
symptoms: This is caused by an incorrect CACHE_ADDR environment variable pointing to a non-existent Valkey instance
errors in the recommendation service logs, 'Valkey connection failed' in application
outcomes: Environment variable or resource limit applied to deployment
Pods restarted and returned to Running state
No further error events in affected namespace
prevention: Use Helm sync waves or init containers to ensure ConfigMaps/Secrets exist before Deployments. Validate RBAC requirements at design time with kubectl auth can-i dry-run checks in CI.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'test-hotel-reservation'. Diagnose and resolve the issue where the 'rate' service is failing to redeploy due to missing Persistent Volume (PV) configurations. Affected: Pods like rate-7bdc978679-r5ckb, namespace test-hotel-reservation, service rate, PVCs and PVs related to mongodb-rate. Symptoms: Events show 'FailedCreateContainer. . Events show. CreateContainerConfigError.
attempted_solutions: kubectl get events -n test-hotel-reservation --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' --field-selector type!=Normal | tail -50
kubectl get deployment rate -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
final_solution:
kubectl apply -f rate-deployment.yaml -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl get pods -n test-hotel-reservation -l app=rate
kubectl describe pod rate-7bdc978679-r5ckb -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl logs rate-7bdc978679-r5ckb -n test-hotel-reservation
reasoning: Root cause: The 'rate' deployment is missing a PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC) reference in its pod specification, causing it to fail during redeployment. This results in the service being unavailable.
symptoms: Error: failed to create container: invalid volume configuration: no PVC found for volume
Error: container has runButNotReady status: ContainerState{Waiting: &ContainerStateWa
outcomes: Kubernetes manifests applied; desired state reconciled
No further error events in affected namespace
prevention: Set resource requests and limits for all containers. Enable VPA or HPA for auto-scaling. Alert at 80% of quota/limit utilisation.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'test-hotel-reservation'. Your task is to diagnose and mitigate the issue where the consul service is not exposing the correct ports, causing connectivity issues with other services. Affected: Pods like consul-78c8d79bb-knhtc, namespace test-hotel-reservation, service consul, pods search-5c64c8f5bc-78db5 and reservation-6f8d456789-2xgkl. Symptoms: The consul service is misconfigured with incorrect port mappings, leading to failed connectivity from other services. Connection refused' when attempting to reach consul:8500. Logs from search pod show 'dial tcp: i/o timeout.
attempted_solutions: kubectl get service consul -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
kubectl get endpoints consul -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
final_solution:
kubectl exec -n test-hotel-reservation search-5c64c8f5bc-78db5 -- nc -zv consul 8500
kubectl logs -n test-hotel-reservation --tail=50 search-5c64c8f5bc-78db5
kubectl get pod consul-78c8d79bb-knhtc -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
kubectl get pod consul-78c8d79bb-knhtc -n test-hotel-reservation -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[0].ports[*].containerPort}'
kubectl get service consul -n test-hotel-reservation -o jsonpath='{.spec.ports[*].port}'
cat > /tmp/consul-service-fixed.yaml << 'EOF'
kubectl delete service consul -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl apply -f /tmp/consul-service-fixed.yaml
kubectl get service consul -n test-hotel-reservation -o wide
kubectl exec -n test-hotel-reservation search-5c64c8f5bc-78db5 -- nc -zv consul 8500
kubectl logs -n test-hotel-reservation --tail=50 search-5c64c8f5bc-78db5
reasoning: Root cause: The consul service is misconfigured with incorrect port mappings, leading to failed connectivity from other services. This results in the search and reservation components being unable to communicate with consul.
symptoms: The consul service is misconfigured with incorrect port mappings, leading to failed connectivity from other services
Connection refused' when attempting to reach consul:8500
Logs from search pod show 'dial tcp: i/o timeout
outcomes: Kubernetes manifests applied; desired state reconciled
No further error events in affected namespace
prevention: Set resource requests and limits for all containers. Enable VPA or HPA for auto-scaling. Alert at 80% of quota/limit utilisation.

problem: Kubernetes Resource fault in namespace 'test-hotel-reservation'. Detect anomalies related to redeploying a service without Persistent Volumes (PVs). Affected: Pods like mongodb-reservation-78c8d79bb-jwtrm, namespace test-hotel-reservation, PVC mongodb-reservation-pvc, service mongodb-reservation. Symptoms: Events show. : invalid mount path. Error: failed to create container: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config f.
attempted_solutions: kubectl get deployment mongodb-reservation -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
final_solution:
kubectl delete pod mongodb-reservation-78c8d79bb-jwtrm -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl apply -f mongodb-reservation-deployment.yaml -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl get pods -n test-hotel-reservation -l app=mongodb-reservation
kubectl describe pod mongodb-reservation-78c8d79bb-jwtrm -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl logs mongodb-reservation-78c8d79bb-jwtrm -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl get pods -n test-hotel-reservation -o wide
kubectl get service mongodb-reservation -n test-hotel-reservation -o wide
reasoning: Root cause: The mongodb-reservation deployment is configured to use a PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC) that does not exist, causing the pods to remain in CrashLoopBackOff due to failed volume mounting. This results in the service being unavailable.
symptoms: Error: failed to create container: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config f
outcomes: Faulty pod deleted and rescheduled by controller
Kubernetes manifests applied; desired state reconciled
No further error events in affected namespace
prevention: Set resource requests and limits for all containers. Enable VPA or HPA for auto-scaling. Alert at 80% of quota/limit utilisation.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'test-hotel-reservation'. Resolve the revoked authentication issue in the MongoDB instance used by the geo service. Affected: Pods like mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-qjcvm, namespace test-hotel-reservation, service mongodb-geo, configmap failure-admin-geo. Symptoms: MongoDB connection error: auth failed', 'Failed to connect to MongoDB: authentication failed. Events show 'CrashLoopBackOff' for the mongodb-geo pod. MongoDB connection error: auth failed.
attempted_solutions: kubectl get configmap failure-admin-geo -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
kubectl get configmap mongo-geo-script -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
kubectl get deployment mongodb-geo -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
final_solution:
kubectl delete pod mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-qjcvm -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl apply -f mongo-geo-credentials.yaml -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl apply -f mongodb-geo-deployment.yaml -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl get pods -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl describe pod mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-qjcvm -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl logs mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-qjcvm -n test-hotel-reservation --tail=100
reasoning: Root cause: The MongoDB instance used by the geo service has had its administrative user revoked, leading to failed connections and data access issues. This results in the geo service being unable to retrieve or update location-based data.
symptoms: MongoDB connection error: auth failed', 'Failed to connect to MongoDB: authentication failed
Events show 'CrashLoopBackOff' for the mongodb-geo pod
Failed to connect to MongoDB: authentication failed
outcomes: Faulty pod deleted and rescheduled by controller
Kubernetes manifests applied; desired state reconciled
No further error events in affected namespace
prevention: Use Helm sync waves or init containers to ensure ConfigMaps/Secrets exist before Deployments. Validate RBAC requirements at design time with kubectl auth can-i dry-run checks in CI.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'test-hotel-reservation'. Your mitigation can be performed using any of the available APIs. Affected: Pods like rate-7bdc978679-nbnw9, mongodb-rate-56cc8659c9-stkfv, namespace test-hotel-reservation, service rate, MongoDB database rate-db. Symptoms: MongoDB: command failed with error 13: 'not authorized on rate-db to execute command { find: "hotels", filter: {. } }' from DB::runCommand. Events show 'Failed to connect to MongoDB instance.
attempted_solutions: kubectl get configmap mongo-profile-script -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
kubectl get deployment mongodb-rate -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
kubectl get configmap mongo-rate-script -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
final_solution:
kubectl exec mongodb-rate-56cc8659c9-stkfv -n test-hotel-reservation -- mongo admin -u admin -p admin --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'db.getUser("admin")'
kubectl exec mongodb-rate-56cc8659c9-stkfv -n test-hotel-reservation -- mongo admin -u admin -p admin --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'db.getUser("admin").roles'
kubectl exec mongodb-rate-56cc8659c9-stkfv -n test-hotel-reservation -- mongo admin -u admin -p admin --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'printjson(db.getUser("admin"))' | grep -A 10 roles
kubectl exec mongodb-rate-56cc8659c9-stkfv -n test-hotel-reservation -- mongo admin -u admin -p admin --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'db.grantRolesToUser("admin", [{role: "readWrite", db: "rate-db"}])'
kubectl get pods -n test-hotel-reservation -l io.kompose.service=rate -o wide
kubectl logs rate-7bdc978679-nbnw9 -n test-hotel-reservation --tail=50
reasoning: Root cause: The MongoDB user 'admin' was revoked access to the 'rate-db' database, causing the rate service to fail when trying to access the database. This results in the rate service being unable to retrieve hotel rates and causing errors in the reservation system.
symptoms: MongoDB: command failed with error 13: 'not authorized on rate-db to execute command { find: "hotels", filter: {
Events show 'Failed to connect to MongoDB instance
outcomes: Fault remediated; service restored to healthy state
No further error events in affected namespace
prevention: Use Helm sync waves or init containers to ensure ConfigMaps/Secrets exist before Deployments. Validate RBAC requirements at design time with kubectl auth can-i dry-run checks in CI.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'test-hotel-reservation'. Detect anomalies related to MongoDB authentication revocation. Affected: Pods like mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-bvmml, namespace test-hotel-reservation, service geo, MongoDB instance geo-db. Symptoms: AtlasError: authentication failed' in geo service logs. MongoDB connection status shows 'auth fails. Events show 'Failed to connect to MongoDB.
final_solution:
kubectl exec -n test-hotel-reservation mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-bvmml -- mongo geo-db -u admin -p admin --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'db.runCommand({connectionStatus: 1})' --quiet
kubectl get secret -n test-hotel-reservation -o name | grep -i mongo
kubectl get secret mongo-geo-credentials -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
kubectl get configmap mongo-geo-script -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml

kubectl exec -n test-hotel-reservation mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-bvmml -- mongo admin -u admin -p admin --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'db.getUser("admin").roles' --quiet
kubectl delete secret mongo-geo-credentials -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl create secret generic mongo-geo-credentials -n test-hotel-reservation --from-literal=MONGO_USER=admin --from-literal=MONGO_PASSWORD=newpassword
kubectl set env deployment/mongodb-geo -n test-hotel-reservation MONGO_PASSWORD=newpassword
kubectl rollout restart deployment/mongodb-geo -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl get pods -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl logs mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-bvmml -n test-hotel-reservation --tail=100
kubectl exec -n test-hotel-reservation mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-bvmml -- mongo geo-db -u admin -p newpassword --authenticationDatabase admin --eval 'db.runCommand({connectionStatus: 1})' --quiet
reasoning: Root cause: The MongoDB admin user 'admin' had its authentication revoked, leading to failed connection attempts from the geo service. This results in the geo service being unable to access the MongoDB database, causing errors in hotel data retrieval.

Events show 'Failed to connect to MongoDB

problem: Kubernetes Scheduling fault in namespace 'test-social-network'. Mitigate the issue where the user-service deployment has zero pods running due to incorrect node affinity settings. Affected: Pods like user-service-5b549cc8dc-4wfmd, namespace test-social-network, service user-service, nodes with labels 'kubernetes. Symptoms: 0/3 nodes are available: 1 node(s) had taints that the pod did not tolerate, 2 node(s) didn. Events show. FailedScheduling.
attempted_solutions: kubectl get pod user-service-5b549cc8dc-4wfmd -n test-social-network -o yaml
final_solution:
kubectl patch deployment user-service -n test-social-network -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"affinity":null}}}}'
kubectl scale deployment user-service -n test-social-network --replicas=2
kubectl get pods -n test-social-network -l service=user-service
kubectl describe pod user-service-5b549cc8dc-4wfmd -n test-social-network
kubectl get pods -n test-social-network
kubectl get events -n test-social-network | grep user-service
reasoning: Root cause: The user-service deployment has a node affinity rule that specifies a non-existent node label, causing pods to remain in Pending state. This results in the service being unavailable.
symptoms: 0/3 nodes are available: 1 node(s) had taints that the pod did not tolerate, 2 node(s) didn
outcomes: Scheduling constraint corrected; pods scheduled successfully
Deployment scaled; pods available
No further error events in affected namespace
prevention: Verify nodeSelector/affinity labels exist on target nodes before deploy. Use PreferredDuringScheduling unless strict isolation is required. Set accurate resource requests so the scheduler can place pods.

problem: Kubernetes Configuration fault in namespace 'test-hotel-reservation'. Your task is to mitigate anomalies detected in the service, specifically related to a user-unregistered MongoDB instance. Affected: Pods like geo-84fbc958c7-krpf9, mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-rctvx, namespace test-hotel-reservation, service geo. Symptoms: MongoDB connection error: auth failed. kubectl get secret mongodb-geo-credentials -n test-hotel-reservation -o jsonpath='{.data.username}' | base64 --decode
final_solution:
kubectl exec mongodb-geo-5ff578bcb8-rctvx -n test-hotel-reservation -- mongosh admin --eval 'db.createUser({user: "geo_user", pwd: "geo_password", roles: [{role: "readWrite", db: "geo"}]})' --quiet
kubectl get configmap mongo-geo-script -n test-hotel-reservation -o yaml
kubectl get deployment geo -n test-hotel-reservation -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].env}' | jq .
kubectl patch deployment geo -n test-hotel-reservation -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"geo","env":[{"name":"MONGO_PASSWORD","value":"geo_password"}]}]}}}}'
kubectl rollout restart deployment/geo -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl get pods -n test-hotel-reservation -l io.kompose.service=geo -o wide
kubectl describe pod geo-84fbc958c7-krpf9 -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl logs geo-84fbc958c7-krpf9 -n test-hotel-reservation --tail=50
reasoning: Root cause: The MongoDB instance 'mongodb-geo' is not registered with the correct user credentials, causing connection failures in the 'geo' service. This results in the geo service being unable to retrieve location data, leading to incomplete hotel recommendations.
symptoms: Failed to authenticate user geo_user on database admin
error: auth failed'; 'Failed to authenticate user geo_user on database admin'; Events
outcomes: Pods restarted and returned to Running state

kubectl logs mongodb-rate-56cc8659c9-qtw5l -n test-hotel-reservation --tail=100
kubectl exec -n test-hotel-reservation mongodb-rate-56cc8659c9-qtw5l -- cat /scripts/failure-admin-rate.sh
kubectl delete secret mongodb-rate-credentials -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl create secret generic mongodb-rate-credentials -n test-hotel-reservation --from-literal=username=admin --from-literal=password=correctpassword
kubectl apply -f mongodb-rate-deployment.yaml -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl get pods -n test-hotel-reservation -l io.kompose.service=mongodb-rate -o wide
kubectl describe pod mongodb-rate-56cc8659c9-qtw5l -n test-hotel-reservation
kubectl logs mongodb-rate-56cc8659c9-qtw5l -n test-hotel-reservation --tail=100

failed for user admin on database admin from client 127
